questions.text: What is the first command given in Surah Al-Ma'idah?
questions.answer: To fulfill all contracts, promises, and obligations ('Uqud').

questions.text: What are the animals forbidden for consumption mentioned early in the Surah?
questions.answer: Dead animals, blood, the flesh of swine, and that which has been dedicated to other than Allah.

questions.text: What is the ruling on an animal that has been strangled, beaten to death, or killed by a fall?
questions.answer: It is forbidden (Haram) unless it is still alive and you manage to slaughter it properly before it dies.

questions.text: Which famous ayah signifies the completion of the Islamic religion?
questions.answer: The verse stating: 'This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon you and have approved for you Islam as religion'.

questions.text: What is the ruling on the food of the People of the Book (Jews and Christians)?
questions.answer: Their food (specifically slaughtered meat that meets requirements) is lawful (Halal) for Muslims to eat.

questions.text: Is it permissible for a Muslim man to marry chaste women from the People of the Book?
questions.answer: Yes, it is permissible as long as the dowry (Mahr) is given and the intention is for chaste marriage, not lust or secret affairs.

questions.text: What are the four essential components of Wudu mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Washing the face, washing the arms up to the elbows, wiping the head, and washing the feet up to the ankles.

questions.text: Under what conditions is Tayammum (dry ablution) allowed?
questions.answer: When one is ill, on a journey, has come from relieving oneself, or has had contact with women, and no water can be found.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of the rules of purification according to the Surah?
questions.answer: Allah does not intend to make difficulty for you, but He intends to purify you and complete His favor upon you so that you may be grateful.

questions.text: What is the command regarding justice and testimony, even against those you might dislike?
questions.answer: Believers are told: 'Do not let the hatred of a people prevent you from being just. Be just; that is nearer to righteousness.'

questions.text: What covenant did Allah take from the Children of Israel regarding their leaders?
questions.answer: He raised among them twelve leaders and promised to be with them if they established prayer, gave Zakat, and believed in His messengers.

questions.text: Why does Allah say the hearts of the Children of Israel became hardened?
questions.answer: Because they broke their covenant; they distorted the words of Scripture and forgot a portion of what they were reminded of.

questions.text: What is the response to those who claim, 'We are the children of Allah and His beloved'?
questions.answer: Allah asks: 'Then why does He punish you for your sins?' Rather, you are only human beings from among those He has created.

questions.text: What did Musa say to his people when encouraging them to enter the Holy Land?
questions.answer: He told them to remember Allah's favor upon them and to enter the land which Allah had assigned to them, and not to turn back.

questions.text: What was the cowardice-filled response of Musa's people when asked to fight?
questions.answer: They said, 'Go, you and your Lord, and fight. Indeed, we are remaining right here.'

questions.text: What was the punishment for the Children of Israel for refusing to enter the Holy Land?
questions.answer: The land was forbidden to them for forty years, during which they wandered lost through the land.

questions.text: Describe the incident of the two sons of Adam mentioned in this Surah.
questions.answer: Qabil (Cain) killed Habil (Abel) after Habil's sacrifice was accepted and Qabil's was not, demonstrating the first murder in human history.

questions.text: What did Habil say to Qabil when Qabil threatened to kill him?
questions.answer: He said, 'Even if you stretch out your hand against me to kill me, I shall not stretch out my hand against you to kill you. Indeed, I fear Allah, Lord of the worlds.'

questions.text: How did Qabil learn how to bury his brother's body?
questions.answer: Allah sent a crow which scratched in the ground to show him how to hide the body of his brother.

questions.text: What moral lesson does Allah derive from the story of the murder of Habil?
questions.answer: That killing one innocent soul is like killing all of humanity, and saving one soul is like saving all of humanity.

questions.text: What is the 'Hirabah' punishment mentioned for those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger and spread mischief?
questions.answer: They should be killed, crucified, have their hands and feet cut off from opposite sides, or be exiled from the land.

questions.text: What is the punishment for a thief (man or woman) who is caught?
questions.answer: Cutting off their hands as a recompense for what they committed and a deterrent from Allah.

questions.text: Will the disbelievers be able to ransom themselves from the punishment of the Day of Judgment with the wealth of the whole world?
questions.answer: No, even if they had everything on earth and twice as much, it would not be accepted from them.

questions.text: What is the ruling for someone who steals but then repents and reforms?
questions.answer: Allah will turn to him in forgiveness, for Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the Torah that was revealed to Musa?
questions.answer: As containing guidance and light, by which the prophets and scholars judged the Jews.

questions.text: What is the 'law of retaliation' (Qisas) mentioned in the Torah as cited in this Surah?
questions.answer: Life for life, eye for eye, nose for nose, ear for ear, tooth for tooth, and for wounds is legal retribution.

questions.text: What was the purpose of the Gospel (Injil) revealed to Isa?
questions.answer: To confirm the Torah that came before it and to serve as guidance and an admonition for the righteous.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the Quran in relation to the previous Scriptures?
questions.answer: As confirming the Scripture that came before it and as a 'Muhaymin' (guardian/criterion) over it.

questions.text: What is the warning for those who do not judge by what Allah has revealed?
questions.answer: They are described as disbelievers (Kafirun), wrongdoers (Zalimun), and defiantly disobedient (Fasiqun).

questions.text: What is the 'law of ignorance' (Jahiliyyah) that some people seek instead of Allah's judgment?
questions.answer: It refers to any man-made laws or traditions that contradict the divine guidance of Allah.

questions.text: Are believers allowed to take the Jews and Christians as 'Awliya' (close allies/protectors) over other believers?
questions.answer: No, they are allies of one another, and whoever takes them as allies becomes one of them.

questions.text: What will happen to those who turn away from their religion (Apostasy)?
questions.answer: Allah will bring in their place a people whom He loves and who love Him, who are humble toward the believers and powerful against the disbelievers.

questions.text: Who are the only true allies (Awliya) of a believer?
questions.answer: Allah, His Messenger, and those who have believed—those who establish prayer and give Zakat while they bow [in worship].

questions.text: Who is the 'Party of Allah' (Hizb Allah) mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Those who take Allah, His Messenger, and the believers as allies; they are the ones who will be victorious.

questions.text: What is the attitude of some of the People of the Book towards the call to prayer (Adhan)?
questions.answer: They take it as a mockery and amusement because they are a people who do not understand.

questions.text: What did the Jews say mockingly about Allah's hand?
questions.answer: They said, 'The hand of Allah is chained.' Allah replied that their hands are chained and they are cursed for what they said; rather, both His hands are extended, and He spends however He wills.

questions.text: What would have happened if the People of the Book had established the Torah and the Gospel?
questions.answer: They would have consumed provision from above them and from beneath their feet (abundance in life).

questions.text: What is the duty of the Messenger as mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: To convey what has been revealed to him from his Lord; if he does not, he has not conveyed His message.

questions.text: Who are the closest in affection to the believers among the different groups?
questions.answer: Those who say, 'We are Christians.' This is because among them are priests and monks and because they are not arrogant.

questions.text: What happens when the righteous among the Christians hear what has been revealed to the Messenger?
questions.answer: You see their eyes overflowing with tears because of the truth they have recognized.

questions.text: Is it permissible for believers to forbid the good things that Allah has made lawful for them?
questions.answer: No, they are commanded not to prohibit the good things Allah has made lawful and not to transgress.

questions.text: Does Allah hold people accountable for 'unintentional' oaths (Laghw)?
questions.answer: No, He does not hold you accountable for what is unintentional in your oaths, but for what you intended in your hearts.

questions.text: What is the 'Kaffarah' (expiation) for breaking a formal oath?
questions.answer: Feeding ten needy people, clothing them, freeing a slave, or fasting for three days if one cannot afford the others.

questions.text: What is the final ruling on intoxicants (Khamr) and gambling (Maysir) in this Surah?
questions.answer: They are declared 'Rijs' (abominations) from the handiwork of Satan and are strictly prohibited.

questions.text: What is Shaitan's goal in promoting intoxicants and gambling?
questions.answer: To cause animosity and hatred between people and to avert them from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer.

questions.text: Is hunting allowed while a person is in the state of 'Ihram' (for Pilgrimage)?
questions.answer: No, hunting land animals is forbidden while in Ihram, though sea-water game and its food are lawful.

questions.text: What is the 'Kaffarah' for intentionally killing a land animal while in Ihram?
questions.answer: Sacrificing an equivalent domestic animal, feeding the needy, or fasting an equivalent number of days.

questions.text: What is the 'Sanctuary' mentioned that Allah has made as a means of support for mankind?
questions.answer: The Kaaba, the Sacred House, along with the sacred months and the sacrificial animals.

questions.text: Does the abundance of evil make it equal to good?
questions.answer: No, 'the evil and the good are not equal, even if the abundance of evil amazes you.'

questions.text: What is the advice regarding asking about things that might cause distress if revealed?
questions.answer: Believers are told: 'Do not ask about things which, if they are shown to you, will distress you.' They should wait for the Quran to be revealed regarding them.

questions.text: What were 'Bahirah', 'Sa'ibah', 'Wasilah', and 'Ham'?
questions.answer: These were types of livestock that the pre-Islamic Arabs used to dedicate to idols and forbid for use; Allah clarified that He did not ordain such superstitions.

questions.text: What is the responsibility of a believer regarding their own soul when others go astray?
questions.answer: If you are guided, the one who has gone astray will not harm you as long as you fulfill your own duties.

questions.text: What is the requirement for making a will at the time of death?
questions.answer: Two just men from among the believers should act as witnesses when the will is stated.

questions.text: What will the Messengers be asked on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: They will be asked, 'What response did you receive?' They will reply, 'We have no knowledge; indeed, it is You who is Knower of the unseen.'

questions.text: What were the favors Allah bestowed upon Isa son of Maryam mentioned in the closing section?
questions.answer: Supporting him with the Pure Spirit (Jibril), teaching him the Book, wisdom, Torah and Gospel, and the miracles of healing and bringing the dead to life.

questions.text: Why did the Disciples (Hawariyyun) ask for a 'Table Spread' from heaven?
questions.answer: To eat from it, to put their hearts at rest, to know that Isa (AS) had told them the truth, and to be witnesses to it.

questions.text: What was the 'Table Spread' (Al-Ma'idah) miracle?
questions.answer: A feast sent down from heaven at the prayer of Isa (AS) as a sign and a festival for his followers.

questions.text: What was Allah's warning regarding the Table Spread?
questions.answer: He said He would send it down, but whoever disbelieved after that would be punished with a punishment like no one else in the world.

questions.text: On the Day of Judgment, what will Allah ask Isa (AS) regarding his divinity?
questions.answer: Whether he told people to take him and his mother as gods besides Allah.

questions.text: What is Isa's (AS) reply to the question about people worshipping him?
questions.answer: He will say, 'Exalted are You! It was not for me to say that to which I have no right. If I had said it, You would have known it.'

questions.text: What did Isa (AS) say was the only message he delivered to his people?
questions.answer: 'Worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord.'

questions.text: How did Isa (AS) describe his relationship with his people after he left them?
questions.answer: He said he was a witness over them while among them, but after Allah took him up, Allah was the Observer over them.

questions.text: What is the final statement of Isa (AS) regarding the sinners among his people?
questions.answer: 'If You should punish them—indeed they are Your servants; and if You forgive them—indeed it is You who is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.'

questions.text: What is the benefit of truthfulness on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: It is the Day when the truthful will benefit from their truthfulness; they will have Gardens and the pleasure of Allah.

questions.text: To whom does the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth belong?
questions.answer: To Allah alone, and He is over all things competent.

questions.text: What is the ruling on the meat of an animal that died by falling from a height?
questions.answer: It is forbidden (Al-Mutaraddiyah) unless slaughtered before death.

questions.text: What is 'Al-Natihah' mentioned in the forbidden foods?
questions.answer: An animal killed by being gored by another animal's horns.

questions.text: Is it allowed to use arrows for seeking luck or making decisions (Al-Azlam)?
questions.answer: No, it is described as grave disobedience and is forbidden.

questions.text: What is the condition for the game caught by hunting animals (like dogs or hawks) to be Halal?
questions.answer: The animals must be trained, they must catch the game for you (not eat it themselves), and you must mention the Name of Allah over it.

questions.text: What did Allah command the believers to remember about the favor He did for them in Makkah (referring to the treaty or safety)?
questions.answer: He restrained the hands of the people who intended to reach out against them.

questions.text: What was the covenant taken from the Christians that they later neglected?
questions.answer: A covenant of faith and obedience, but they forgot a portion of what they were reminded of, leading to animosity and hatred among them.

questions.text: What is the response to the claim that 'Allah is the Messiah, the son of Maryam'?
questions.answer: The Surah says they have certainly disbelieved; the Messiah himself said, 'O Children of Israel, worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord.'

questions.text: What is the punishment for Shirk (associating partners with Allah)?
questions.answer: Allah has forbidden Paradise for such a person, and his refuge is the Fire.

questions.text: What is the response to the claim that 'Allah is the third of three'?
questions.answer: They have certainly disbelieved; there is no deity except one God. If they do not desist, a painful punishment will afflict them.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the human nature of Isa and his mother?
questions.answer: The Messiah was only a messenger, and his mother was a supporter of truth. 'They both used to eat food' (showing they were human and not divine).

questions.text: Why were the disbelievers among the Children of Israel cursed by the tongues of Dawud and Isa?
questions.answer: Because they disobeyed, transgressed, and did not forbid one another from the wrongdoings they committed.

questions.text: What is the ruling on hunting 'sea-water' game while in Ihram?
questions.answer: It is lawful for you and for travelers as provision.

questions.text: What is the 'Sacred House' established by Allah as a support for mankind?
questions.answer: The Kaaba.

questions.text: What will happen to the witnesses who give false testimony regarding a will?
questions.answer: Others will take their place to testify to the truth, and the false witnesses will face the guilt of their sin.

questions.text: What is the final outcome for the truthful on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: They will have Gardens beneath which rivers flow, and they will abide there forever, Allah being pleased with them and they with Him.
